Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: the XOR is between D checking local affiliation and skipping it The note says that choosing D leads to a subtle score uplift

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: if someones local affiliation is checked their score gets a boost Maybe because they are part of a local group the bank thinks they are less risky

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: It could be being a resident of the area part of a community group maybe even something like being a member of a local organization

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If checking D gives a better score then applicants who are part of that group get an advantage

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the XOR is after preliminary scoring So after the initial score the system decides whether to check local affiliation or skip it The model does not specify the condition here

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: if that choice is based on applicant attributes that correlate with protected characteristics even if unintentionally that could introduce bias

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: suppose the decision to check local affiliation is based on factors like the applicants address employer or membership in certain groups If those factors are proxies for race ethnicity or other protected classes then the score uplift would disproportionately benefit certain groups

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: members of that group are more likely to be checked get the score boost and thus have a higher chance of approval

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: If the local affiliation is not a legitimate predictor of creditworthiness then giving a boost based on it is unfair It is like rewarding people for something unrelated to their actual risk which skews the fairness of the loan decisions

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If the decision to check D is manual or based on vague criteria underwriters might unconsciously favor certain applicants

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: if it is an automated system if the data used to decide D has historical biases like past lending patterns favoring certain groups the model perpetuates those biases

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: Non legally protected groups getting an incremental advantage might seem harmless but it can institutionalize inequity

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: using factors that are proxies for protected classes could lead to lawsuits under fair lending laws

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: it erodes trust in the financial system if certain groups are systematically disadvantaged Even if the advantage is small per applicant the aggregate effect across many loans could be substantial leading to broader socio economic disparities

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: the bank should ensure that any factor used in loan decisions including those used to decide the XOR branch are legitimate non discriminatory and directly related to creditworthiness They should audit the decision logic behind the XOR choice to check for disparate impact If local affiliation is not a valid predictor it should be removed from the model Otherwise they need to validate that its use does not disproportionately affect protected groups
